name: Roast Goose with Molasses Glaze and Apricot Stuffing
recipeIngredient: Fresh thyme, chopped -OR- dried thyme (1 teaspoon)
Sage, chopped -OR- dried rubbed sage (1/2 teaspoon)
recipeInstructions:
1. To make molasses glaze: In a medium bowl, combine molasses, bottled hot pepper sauce, ginger, garlic, and coarsely ground black pepper. Mix well and set aside.
2. To prepare the roast goose: Preheat oven to 425°F. Remove giblets from the goose. Rinse the goose in cold water, removing as much fat as possible from the body cavity. Pat the goose dry with paper towels. Pierce the skin all over with the tines of a large fork. Season the goose inside and out with salt.
3. Place the goose breast-side up on a rack in a large shallow roasting pan. Roast at 425°F for 15 minutes. Reduce the oven temperature to 350°F. Continue roasting for 20 to 23 minutes per pound, or until a thermometer inserted between the leg and thickest part of the thigh registers 180°F (make sure the thermometer does not touch bone).
4. Drain off all fat from the pan. Brush some of the molasses glaze over the goose. Continue roasting for an additional 20 minutes, brushing with the molasses mixture after 10 minutes.
5. Remove the goose from the oven. Cover with foil and let stand 15 minutes before carving.
6. To make apricot stuffing: In a deep 1-1/2 quart casserole, butter. In a medium skillet, sauté onion and celery in hot oil over medium-high heat until soft, about 4 to 5 minutes. Pour the onion mixture into a large bowl.
7. Add garlic, cracked pepper, thyme, parsley, sage, apricots, cornbread, and salt to taste. Toss to mix. Set aside.
8. In a small bowl, whisk together egg and stock. Pour over the stuffing. Mix well. (The mixture will seem soupy.) Pour into the baking dish. Bake at 350°F for 40 minutes, or until hot in the center.
9. To serve: Arrange the goose on a serving platter. Garnish with apples and grapes. Carve the goose. Serve with apricot stuffing.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings.
